summary.intent: Address PR review comments on a GPG release-signing feature (PR #525), then iteratively improve the verification script based on subsequent reviewer feedback about security gaps.
summary.outcome: All review threads resolved across multiple review rounds; a `verify-release.sh` script was created and hardened (GPG output suppression fixed, fingerprint pinning added); two-phase release workflow documented in CLAUDE.md.

repo: Release workflow is two-phase: Phase 1 is prepare/tag/push (CI builds artifacts), Phase 2 is GPG-signing with YubiKey via gpg-sign-release.sh after CI finishes — agents must not attempt to sign in the same session as tagging.
CLAUDE.md contains the authoritative release workflow documentation and agent instructions.
Greptile posts a summary comment with a confidence score (not a review thread) — this is separate from resolvable review threads and must be checked independently.
GPG key for the maintainer is published at https://pen.so/gpg.asc

code.finding: gpg --batch --verify ... 2>/dev/null suppresses both the 'Good signature from ...' identity line on success and all diagnostics on failure — always capture and surface GPG stderr.

Under set -euo pipefail, piping gpg --import output through grep will abort the script when GPG emits lines (new signatures, subkeys) that don't match the filter — use || true or capture output instead.
Fetching a GPG key from a URL without pinning the expected fingerprint is a TOFU (trust-on-first-use) vulnerability — always verify the imported key's fingerprint matches a hardcoded expected value.
SHA256 verification must have an explicit else branch warning to stderr when no checksum file is found — silent skips hide integrity gaps.
--clobber behavior should query existing .asc files on the release and print a NOTE before overwriting, rather than silently replacing.

workflow: Greptile review comments appear as a summary issue-level comment with a confidence score, not as resolvable review threads — requires a separate gh api call to surface.
When checking for 'new comments', must check both unresolved review threads (GraphQL) AND issue-level comments and review bodies by timestamp to catch Greptile-style summary comments.
Resolving PR conversations requires individual GraphQL mutations per thread ID, not a bulk operation.

friction: Multiple 'Fix new comments' requests returned false negatives (no new comments) because the Greptile summary confidence score lives in an issue comment, not a review thread — the standard unresolved-thread query missed it.

Iterative back-and-forth with the reviewer (real or automated) required 5+ separate fix/push/resolve cycles for a single PR, suggesting the initial implementation missed several security fundamentals.
open_items: docs/src/release-verification.md was modified to add the pinned fingerprint but the commit was cut off in the transcript — confirm it was pushed.
The expected GPG fingerprint is now hardcoded in verify-release.sh and docs — needs to be updated if the maintainer rotates their key.
Greptile confidence score history not tracked — unclear if pinning the fingerprint brings the score to 5/5 or if further issues remain.
